title: Slow Mediterranean Lamb Bake
description: A hearty, comforting slow-cooked lamb dish inspired by Mediterranean flavors, perfect for a sick day or a cozy evening in.
instructions: Season the lamb shanks with salt and black pepper.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Sear the lamb shanks until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Add the onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, oregano, and rosemary to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the red wine, chicken broth, tomato paste, salt, and pepper to the pot. Stir to combine.
Return the lamb to the pot, cover with a lid, and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and falling off the bone.
Remove the pot from the oven and brush the lamb with lemon juice.
Let the lamb rest for 10-15 minutes before serving.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
